title: HTTP Caching, Freshness, Validators, Vary, and Invalidation · Go Client Systems · Language Lens: Vary Cache Key
summary: A metadata synchronizer uses ETags and shared gateways but has served a private stale representation after a Vary mistake. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http go vary cache key increment in a Go 1.26 net/http spec-aware cache decision notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Construct cache keys that respect method, target URI, Vary fields, authorization policy, and representation selection. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Use ETag and Last-Modified validators with If-None-Match or If-Modified-Since and interpret 304 correctly. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Predict Vary Cache Key · Theory Vary Cache Key
steps.instruction:
theory case — Go fleet evidence model stores a response with max-age=20, ETag "rev-2", Age:2, and Vary: Accept-Language. A user-specific request arrives stale; compute reuse, revalidation, 304 metadata merge, cache key, and privacy policy. Competency probe: Construct cache keys that respect method, target URI, Vary fields, authorization policy, and representation selection. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
